report_metadata.report_title: CYBERSECURITY ADVISORY Countering Chinese State-Sponsored Actors Compromise of Networks to Feed Global Espionage System

ip_addresses.evidence_snippet: The following IP indicators were associated with the APT actors’ activity from August 2021 to June 2025.
The following IP indicators were associated with the APT actors’ activity from August 2021 to June 2025.
The following IP indicators were associated with the APT actors’ activity from August 2021 to June 2025.

tools.name: STOWAWAY
tools.evidence_snippet: Leveraging open source multi-hop pivoting tools, such as STOWAWAY, to build chained relays for command and control (C2) and operator access

tools.name: cmd3 SFTP client
tools.evidence_snippet: The APT actors also use a custom SFTP client, which is a Linux binary written in Golang, to transfer encrypted archives from one location to another.

cves.evidence_snippet: Exploiting CVE-2024-21887 - Ivanti Connect Secure and Ivanti Policy Secure web-component command injection vulnerability
Exploiting CVE-2024-3400 - Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect arbitrary file creation leading to OS command injection
Exploiting CVE-2023-20273 - Cisco Internetworking Operating System (IOS) XE software web management user interface post-authentication command injection/privilege escalation

ttps.evidence_snippet: The APT actors are having considerable success exploiting publicly known common vulnerabilities and exposures (CVEs) and other avoidable weaknesses within compromised infrastructure.
The APT actors often modify router configurations for lateral movement pivoting between networks and using virtualized containers on network devices to evade detection.

ttps.technique: Command and Control: Standard Command and Control (C2) Tool

summary: The APT actors are targeting networks globally, including telecommunications, government, transportation, and lodging infrastructure. They exploit publicly known CVEs and other weaknesses to gain initial access.
